english: heavens Cs. — mk'a-'gro-ma (mkha' 'gro ma), in Mil. gen. mk'a-p'ro-ma (mkha' phro ma), Ssk. डाकिनी, fabulous beings of more modern legends, 'wise' women of supernatural powers, sometimes represented like angels, at other times like fairies or witches. — mk'a-mnyám (mkha' mnyam) Lex. like the heavens, infinite. — mk'a-ldiṅ (mkha' lding) the sky-soarer, the bird Garuda, v. k'yuṅ (khyung). — mk'a-spyód (mkha' spyod) wanderings through heaven Tar. 112, 4, also: enjoyment of heaven, enjoying or inhabiting heaven; mk'a-spyód-du gśégs-pa (mkha' spyod du gshegs pa) to go to heaven, to die Mil.

1. resp. sku-mk'ár (sku mkhar). Glr., castle, nobleman's seat or mansion, manor-house, frq.; citadel, fort Pth.; house in general Mil. — 2. termin. case of mk'a (mkha'). Comp. — mk'ar-dpón (mkhar dpon) governor of a castle, commander of a fortress. — mk'ar-lás (mkhar las) C. and B., the work of constructing a castle, of raising an edifice; *k'ar-léṅ* (khar leng) W. id. — mk'ar-srúṅ (mkhar srung) the guard or garrison of a castle, fortress Cs.

1. (also k'ár-ba (khar ba)) B. and C., staff, stick; mk'ar-gsil (mkhar gsil) staff of the mendicant friars, the upper part of which is hung with jingling rings; p'yag-mk'ár (phyag mkhar) resp. for mk'ár-ba (mkhar ba). — 2. bronze, bell-metal, v. k'ár-ba (khar ba).
kidney, reins, mk'al mdog (mkhal mdog) 'kidney-colour, dark red' Cs.

Ssk. पटु, (originally like σοφός) skilled; skilful, in mechanical work, and so it is frq. used in col. language; further in a more general sense: smán-pa mk'ás-pa (sman pa mkhas pa) a skilful, clever physician; experienced, learned, prudent, shrewd, wise; c. accus. or dat., in a thing; so-ts'is-la (so tshis la) in farming, č'os (chos) in religion; slób-ma skyóṅ-ba-la mk'ás-pa (slob ma skyong ba la mkhas pa) an able tutor, pedagogue Mil.; mk'as-btsun-bzáṅ (mkhas btsun bzang) prop. denotes the qualities of a right priest: learned, conscientious, good, but sometimes it indicates only the position in society, the clerical rank, so esp. mk'as-btsún (mkhas btsun) learned clerics, reverends Glr., mk'ás-po (mkhas po) or -pa a learned man, a scholar, sṅón-gyi mk'ás-po-rnams (sngon gyi mkhas po rnams) learned men of former times; mk'as-grúb (mkhas grub) id., rgya-gár-gyi mk'as-grúb-rnams (rgya gar gyi mkhas grub rnams) Indian scholars; it is also used like our 'most wise', 'very learned', and similar expressions in a pompous address Glr.; mk'as-mč'óg (mkhas mchog) profound scholar Zam. I never found the word applied to inanimate things in the sense of 'wisely contrived', and the correctness of Cs.: t'abs mk'ás-pos (thabs mkhas pos) 'by wise means' may be questioned.

desirable, to be wished for, mk'ó-bai yo-byád (mkho ba'i yo byad), in C. also *k'o-jhe* (kho jhe), desirable things, requisits, wants, desiderata; *hińdu-t'ín-gyi mi-la k'ó-we tsoṅ-zóg* (hindu thin gyi mi la kho we tsong zog) articles of commerce, goods, commodities, such as they are called for in Hindostan; nyé-bar mk'ó-ba (nye bar mkho ba) indispensable, most necessary. Cf. k'o (kho).

Cs.: to keep, to hold, to embrace, = k'yúd-pa (khyud pa); dpe-mk'yúd (dpe mkhyud) Lex. w.e.; Cs.: unwillingness to lend books, dpe-mk'yúd-čan (dpe mkhyud can) unwilling to lend books, dpe-mk'yúd byed-pa (dpe mkhyud byed pa) to be unwilling to lend books; mk'yud-spyád (mkhyud spyad) a sort of bag or vessel for carrying something (?); sorcery, witchcraft Sch.

resp. for śés-pa (shes pa), ríg-pa (rig pa), gó-ba (go ba), to know, yab-yúm-gyis mk'yén-par mdzód-čig (yab yum gyis mkhyen par mdzod cig) my esteemed parents may know Dzl.; to know, one man from another, rgyál-po mk'yén-tam (rgyal po mkhyen tam) does the king, does your majesty know the ... ? (the king himself will answer: ṅas śés-so (ngas shes so)) Dzl. It is frq. used of the supernatural perception of Buddha and the saints, bsám-pa dág-par mk'yén-pas (bsam pa dag par mkhyen pas) as he (the Bodhisattwa) perceived the sentiments (of his scholar) to be sincere Dzl.; mk'yén-par gyúr-to (mkhyen par gyur to) perceived, found out, discovered; to understand, mk'yen sóṅ-ṅam (mkhyen song ngam) did you understand it? mk'yen-rgyá-čan (mkhyen rgya can) possessed of much understanding, very learned Mil.; mk'yén-ldan-yáṅs-pa (mkhyen ldan yangs pa) profoundly learned; mk'yen-brtsé (mkhyen brtse) Glr. prob.: omniscient-merciful; t'ams-čad-mk'yén (thams cad mkhyen) all-knowing, a later epi-
